perturbation.original_text: 1.3 Additional Fee. As an additional fee, the Operating Company shall also pay to EmployeeCo an amount equal to 1% of the amount of all reimbursements made under Section 1.2, payable at the same time as such reimbursements, unless otherwise agreed between the Operating Company and EmployeeCo.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 Additional Fee. As an additional fee, the Operating Company *may* pay to EmployeeCo an amount *to be determined* of all reimbursements made under Section 1.2, payable at the same time as such reimbursements, unless otherwise agreed between the Operating Company and EmployeeCo.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'may' and 'equal to 1%' to 'to be determined', the obligation for the Operating Company to pay a fixed additional fee becomes discretionary and undefined. This introduces uncertainty, potentially contradicting the intent of Section 1.2 and any expectations of consistent reimbursement practices, since it references a fee that now might not exist, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 Reimbursement and Allocation. The Operating Company shall reimburse EmployeeCo for all direct or indirect costs and expenses incurred by EmployeeCo in connection with performing its obligations under this Agreement including, but not limited to: (a) salaries of the Services Employees; (b) the cost of employee benefits for the Services Employees, including 401(k), pension, bonuses and health insurance benefits (whether through insurance policies provided by third-parties or self-insurance); (c) costs associated with workers' compensation claims and other disputes or liabilities associated with the Services Employees; (d) severance costs with respect to any terminated Services Employees; and (e) all sales, use, employment, excise, value added or similar taxes, if any, that may be applicable from time to time with respect to the Services. Such reimbursements shall be made by the Operating Company to EmployeeCo in advance or immediately upon such costs being incurred, or otherwise in accordance with historical practice, unless otherwise agreed between the Operating Company and EmployeeCo.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated an 'additional fee' of '1%' based on Section 1.2. The changed text makes the fee discretionary ('may pay') and undefined ('to be determined'). This contradicts Section 1.2, which outlines specific reimbursements. The changed text introduces uncertainty about whether the 'additional fee' will be paid at all, creating a direct conflict with the expectation of reimbursement within the contract, thus qualifying as an 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Termination of Agreement. This Agreement shall remain in effect until terminated by the Parties. This Agreement may be terminated by (a) the written agreement of the Parties or (b) by either Party upon 5 days written notice to the other Party. All payment obligations hereunder shall survive the termination of this Agreement in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Termination of Agreement. This Agreement shall remain in effect until terminated by the Parties. This Agreement may be terminated by (a) the written agreement of the Parties. All payment obligations hereunder shall survive the termination of this Agreement in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (b), which allows termination by either party with 5 days written notice, the only method for termination becomes mutual written agreement. This contradicts the original implication that a single party could initiate termination, creating an in-text contradiction. The removal of this clause raises the question of whether unilateral termination is still possible, since there is no other clauses in the contract indicating such possibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Termination of Agreement. This Agreement shall remain in effect until terminated by the Parties. This Agreement may be terminated by (a) the written agreement of the Parties. All payment obligations hereunder shall survive the termination of this Agreement in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the clause allowing either party to terminate the agreement with 5 days written notice. This directly contradicts the original clause, as it eliminates a previously available termination method, creating an inconsistency regarding how the agreement can be terminated. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, indicating that the omission from the original text creates a contradiction by restricting the means of termination within the same section of the document.
